title: FAA Proposes Petition Process for Drone Restrictions Over Critical Infrastructure
excerpt: The FAA proposed a rule that would let eligible critical infrastructure operators petition for drone flight restrictions over sensitive sites, reflecting continued policy movement around facility protection and low-altitude airspace security.

title: DroneShield and Terma Sign MOU for Layered Counter-UAS Capabilities
excerpt: DroneShield and Terma announced a strategic collaboration focused on interoperable, layered counter-UAS capabilities combining AI-enabled detection, electronic warfare systems, air-defense technologies, and command-and-control software.

title: DroneShield and Overland AI Link Counter-UAS Sensors with Autonomous Ground Protection
excerpt: DroneShield announced interoperability between DroneSentry-X Mk2 and Overland AI's ULTRA unmanned ground vehicle, highlighting a trend toward mobile counter-UAS coverage for autonomous ground operations.

title: U.S. Army Awards Anduril Enterprise Contract for Commercial Defense Technologies
excerpt: The U.S. Army awarded Anduril an enterprise contract framework intended to consolidate procurement of commercial technologies and accelerate fielding of software, hardware, data infrastructure, and technical support capabilities.

title: Epirus and Digital Force Technologies Partner on Non-Kinetic Counter-UAS Kill Chain
excerpt: Epirus and Digital Force Technologies announced an integrated counter-UAS approach combining DFT's Seraphim command-and-control and sensor fusion software with Epirus' Leonidas high-power microwave platform.

title: FAA and FBI Establish No Drone Zones for Super Bowl LX
excerpt: The FAA and FBI announced temporary drone restrictions around Super Bowl LX venues, with federally authorized teams deployed to detect, track, assess, and respond to unauthorized drone activity near event locations.

title: FAA Schedules Drone Detection Testing Near Santa Teresa, New Mexico
excerpt: The FAA announced off-airport drone-detection testing near Santa Teresa, New Mexico, with ASSURE, Customs and Border Protection, local first responders, and around 100 commercial off-the-shelf drones participating.

title: Newark Liberty Airport Closes Runways Repeatedly Due to Drone Incursions
excerpt: Newark Liberty experienced multiple runway closures over several weeks due to drone sightings in restricted airspace, renewing pressure on the FAA to accelerate counter-UAS deployment at major airports.

title: DJI Remains on NDAA Prohibited List; US Military Continues Alternative Sourcing
excerpt: Despite lobbying efforts, DJI remains designated as a covered entity under the NDAA, blocking US government procurement. American manufacturers including Skydio and Joby continue to fill the gap.

title: Ukraine FPV Drone Production Reaches Industrial Scale; Electronic Countermeasures Evolve in Response
excerpt: Ukrainian forces are producing tens of thousands of FPV drones monthly. Russian electronic warfare units have adapted, deploying mobile jamming systems closer to the front to counter the threat, while Ukraine develops frequency-hopping and AI-guided autonomous variants.

title: FAA Expands Counter-UAS Pilot Program to 10 Additional Airports
excerpt: The FAA announced expansion of its counter-UAS technology evaluation program to 10 additional US airports, following positive results from the initial 5-site deployment. The program tests RF detection, radar, and integrated C-UAS management systems.

title: HENSOLDT Receives Major Xpeller C-UAS Order from European Government
excerpt: HENSOLDT AG announced a significant contract for its Xpeller counter-drone system, which integrates radar, RF sensing, and jamming capabilities into a single platform. Financial terms were not disclosed.

title: CISA Releases Updated Counter-UAS Framework for Critical Infrastructure Operators
excerpt: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency released updated guidance for critical infrastructure operators on counter-UAS deployment, including a tiered response framework and coordination procedures with federal law enforcement.

title: Skydio Wins $100M+ DoD Contract for Autonomous UAS Program
excerpt: Skydio has been awarded a significant Department of Defense contract for its autonomous drone systems, part of the DoD's effort to source drones from non-NDAA-prohibited manufacturers.

title: Houthi Maritime Drone Attacks Drive Demand for Naval C-UAS Systems
excerpt: Continued Houthi drone and USV attacks on commercial shipping in the Red Sea have driven significant interest in naval counter-UAS systems, with multiple navies accelerating procurement of directed-energy and RF jamming solutions for shipboard defense.

title: Dedrone Acquired; C-UAS Software Market Consolidates
excerpt: The counter-drone software sector continued consolidation as Dedrone was acquired, combining its RF detection and airspace management platform with a larger defense electronics portfolio. Analysts expect further M&A activity as the market matures.

title: UK Home Office Expands C-UAS Authorization to Additional Airport Categories
excerpt: The UK Home Office updated its counter-UAS authorization framework, expanding eligibility for active countermeasure deployment to regional airports and major event venues under a new licensed operator scheme.

title: US Marine Corps Deploys First Operational Laser Counter-Drone System
excerpt: The Marine Corps has fielded its first operational directed-energy counter-UAS system, a compact laser unit designed for expeditionary use. The system can engage Group 1 and Group 2 drones at ranges up to 1 km.

title: India Accelerates C-UAS Procurement After Border Drone Incidents
excerpt: India's Ministry of Home Affairs fast-tracked procurement of counter-drone systems for deployment along the Pakistan border following a series of drone-dropped weapon and narcotics incidents. BEL and foreign vendors are competing for the contract.

title: GNSS Spoofing Attacks Target Precision Agriculture; Losses Exceed $50M in 2025
excerpt: Academic researchers have documented a rise in GPS/RTK spoofing attacks against precision agriculture drones, with farmers reporting incorrect crop treatment due to false positioning. Defense researchers warn the same techniques could target military UAVs with RTK payloads.

title: New ML-Based RF Fingerprinting Achieves 96% Drone Classification Accuracy Without Signal Decryption
excerpt: Researchers at multiple universities have published results on RF fingerprinting techniques that identify drone models by physical-layer signal characteristics alone—enabling counter-UAS systems to classify encrypted drones without protocol analysis.

title: NATO Standardizes Multi-Band Counter-UAS Jamming Requirements for Allied Force Protection
excerpt: NATO released updated technical standards for multi-band RF counter-drone systems, specifying simultaneous 2.4/5.1/5.8 GHz coverage as a baseline requirement. New standards reflect increased capability of modern drones like DJI O4.

title: DJI Phantom 4 RTK Dominates Industrial Survey Market; RTK Drones Represent 40% of Enterprise Purchases
excerpt: Market analysis shows that RTK-equipped drones now represent 40% of enterprise UAV procurement, with DJI's Phantom 4 RTK holding 65% market share in the precision agriculture and surveying verticals. Adoption driven by sub-5cm positioning accuracy.

title: Federal Courts Rule that RF Jamming Residue Can Be Used as Forensic Evidence Against Unauthorized Countermeasures
excerpt: A landmark federal ruling allows prosecutors to use RF spectral evidence of unauthorized jamming as admissible evidence in criminal cases. The decision has implications for both law enforcement using C-UAS systems and private entities attempting to counter unauthorized drone flights.
